key_insight: A transfer-learning plus hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász optimization makes BiSeNetV2 much more robust to reflective weld seams without adding model complexity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical real-time perception upgrade for autonomous welding robots operating on shiny, low-contrast construction surfaces.
method: Fine-tuned a BiSeNetV2 backbone with transfer learning.
Used a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss to stabilize learning on thin seam boundaries.
Compared against BiSeNetV2, DeepLabV3+, UNet, and SegFormer under reflective conditions.
evidence_signal: The method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while keeping FLOPs, parameters, and inference speed unchanged.
watch_item: Performance gains are shown on the reported dataset and conditions, so deployment may still need site-specific validation for new metals, lighting, and camera setups.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
